mission.title: Install and Configure Oras on Kubernetes
mission.description: ORAS is an OCI registry client that allows you to manage content like artifacts, images, and packages. Installing ORAS enables you to interact with OCI-compliant registries effectively.

steps.title: Add the ORAS Helm repository
steps.description:
To install ORAS using Helm, first add the ORAS Helm repository:
```bash
helm repo add oras https://oras.land/helm-charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install ORAS using Helm
steps.description:
Now, install ORAS with the following command, specifying the version and creating a namespace:
```bash
helm install oras oras/oras --version v1.3.0 --namespace oras --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the ORAS pods are running successfully:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace oras
```

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
You may want to configure resource limits for the ORAS deployment. Edit the deployment with:
```bash
kubectl edit deployment oras --namespace oras
``` 
Add resource limits under the containers section:
```yaml
resources:
  limits:
    memory: "512Mi"
    cpu: "500m"
```

steps.title: Access ORAS CLI
steps.description:
To use the ORAS CLI, you can port-forward the service:
```bash
kubectl port-forward service/oras --namespace oras 8080:80
``` 
You can now access the ORAS CLI at http://localhost:8080.

resolution.summary: A successful installation will have the ORAS pods running in the 'oras' namespace. You can verify the installation by checking the pod status and accessing the ORAS CLI through port forwarding.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add oras https://oras.land/helm-charts
helm repo update
helm install oras oras/oras --version v1.3.0 --namespace oras --create-namespace

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
To cleanly remove the ORAS deployment, use the following command to uninstall the Helm release:
```bash
helm uninstall oras --namespace oras
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and persistent resources
uninstall.description:
If there are any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) or persistent volumes associated with ORAS, you can remove them with:
```bash
kubectl delete crd <crd-name>
kubectl delete pvc --all --namespace oras
```

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description:
Finally, delete the namespace and verify that it has been removed:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace oras
kubectl get namespaces
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, it's a good practice to back up the current state of your ORAS deployment. You can do this by exporting the current configuration:
```bash
kubectl get deployment oras --namespace oras -o yaml > oras-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update repo and run upgrade command
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository and upgrade ORAS to the latest version:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade oras oras/oras --version v1.4.0 --namespace oras
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
After the upgrade, check the status of the ORAS pods to ensure they are running correctly:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace oras
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If the ORAS pods are in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for more information:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace oras
``` 
Look for any error messages that indicate what might be wrong.

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access the ORAS CLI, ensure that the service is running and the port-forwarding is set up correctly:
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace oras
kubectl port-forward service/oras --namespace oras 8080:80
```

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient resources allocated
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter issues related to resource limits, check the resource usage of the pods:
```bash
kubectl top pods --namespace oras
``` 
Consider adjusting the resource limits in the deployment if necessary.

troubleshooting.title: Helm upgrade fails with version conflict
troubleshooting.description:
If the Helm upgrade fails due to a version conflict, check the installed version and the available versions:
```bash
helm list --namespace oras
helm search repo oras/oras --versions
``` 
Make sure you are specifying a valid version in your upgrade command.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and both Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
